Andretti was named Driver of the Century by the [[Associated Press]] (1999) and RACER magazine (2000).<ref name="AHOF–2025" /><ref name="International Recognition">{{Cite web |title=International Recognition |url=https://www.marioandretti.com/international-recognition |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=MarioAndretti.com |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411194554/https://www.marioandretti.com/international-recognition |url-status=live }}</ref> In 1992, he was voted the U.S. Driver of the Quarter Century by a panel of journalists and former U.S. Drivers of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Martin |first=Bruce |date=1992-10-06 |title=Mario Andretti wins Driver of the Quarter Century |url=https://www.upi.com/Archives/1992/10/06/Mario-Andretti-wins-Driver-of-the-Quarter-Century/6823718344000/ |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=UPI |language=en |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411194545/https://www.upi.com/Archives/1992/10/06/Mario-Andretti-wins-Driver-of-the-Quarter-Century/6823718344000/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=1992-10-07 |title=Mario Driver of the Quarter Century |url=https://www.mcall.com/1992/10/07/mario-driver-of-the-quarter-century/?amp=1 |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=[[The Morning Call]] |language=en-US}}</ref> He was named the U.S. Driver of the Year in 1967, 1978, and 1984,<ref>{{cite web |date=January 25, 2005 |title=Greg Anderson named 2004 SPEED Channel driver of the year |url=http://www.redlineoil.com/news_article.asp?id=204 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20061016015751/http://www.redlineoil.com/news_article.asp?id=204 |archive-date=October 16, 2006 |access-date=July 11, 2007 |work=Red Line Synthetic Oil}}</ref> and is the only driver to be Driver of the Year in three decades.<ref name="ESPN" />

Andretti has been inducted into a variety of motorsports hall of fames, including the [[International Motorsports Hall of Fame]] in 2000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Biography |url=http://www.motorsportshalloffame.com/halloffame/2000/Mario_Andretti_main.htm |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060508043942/http://www.motorsportshalloffame.com/halloffame/2000/Mario_Andretti_main.htm |archive-date=May 8, 2006 |access-date=February 22, 2007 |work=[[International Motorsports Hall of Fame]]}}</ref> Other halls of fame include the [[Auto Racing Hall of Fame|Indianapolis Motor Speedway Hall of Fame]] (1986),<ref name="International Recognition" /> the [[Motorsports Hall of Fame of America]] (1990),<ref name="MSHoF">[http://www.mshf.com/hall-of-fame/inductees/mario-andretti.html Mario Andretti] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191113045829/https://www.mshf.com/hall-of-fame/inductees/mario-andretti.html |date=November 13, 2019 }} at the [[Motorsports Hall of Fame of America]]</ref> the U.S. [[National Sprint Car Hall of Fame & Museum|National Sprint Car Hall of Fame]] (1996),<ref name="NSCHoF" /> the [[Automotive Hall of Fame]] (2005),<ref>{{cite web|title=Automotive Hall of Fame Announces 2005 Inductees|url=https://www.aftermarketnews.com/automotive-hall-of-fame-announces-2005-inductees/|work=Aftermarket News|date=March 24, 2005|access-date=July 30, 2021|archive-date=July 30, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210730150924/https://www.aftermarketnews.com/automotive-hall-of-fame-announces-2005-inductees/|url-status=live}}</ref> the [[United States Auto Club|USAC]] Hall of Fame (2012),<ref>{{Cite web |title=Mario Andretti {{!}} USAC Hall of Fame Inductee |url=https://www.usacracing.com/hall-of-fame-inductees/mario-andretti |access-date=2025-02-01 |website=United States Auto Club |language=en}}</ref> the [[FIA Hall of Fame]] (2017),<ref>{{Cite web |title=FIA Hall of Fame |url=https://halloffame.fia.com/enshrinement/2017 |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=Federation Internationale de l'Automobile |archive-date=March 12, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240312181006/https://halloffame.fia.com/enshrinement/2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref> and the U.S. [[National Midget Auto Racing Hall of Fame]] (2019).<ref name="International Recognition" />

Various race tracks have named areas after Andretti, including "The Andretti" (the final turn of the [[Circuit of the Americas]]),<ref>{{Cite web |last=Medland |first=Chris |date=2022-10-17 |title=COTA to name final turn 'The Andretti' |url=https://racer.com/2022/10/17/cota-to-name-final-turn-the-andretti/ |access-date=2025-01-30 |website=[[Racer (magazine)|RACER]] |language=en-US (turn 2 at [[Laguna Seca]]),<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ward |first=Sean |date=2022-08-11 |title=Everything you need to know about Laguna Seca grandstand driveway at [[Pocono Raceway|Pocono]]).<ref>{{Cite web |last=Augugliaro |first=Max |date=2024-07-08 |title=NASCAR weekend: Route 115 is one-way before, after Great American Getaway 400 on Sunday |url=https://www.poconorecord.com/story/news/traffic/2024/07/08/traffic-parking-route-115-nascar-weekend-pocono-raceway/74311519007/ |access-date=2025-01-31 |website=Pocono Record }}</ref> [[Indianapolis]] renamed a portion of a street "Mario Andretti Drive" in 2019 to celebrate the 50th anniversary of his [[1969 Indianapolis 500]] win.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Robinson |first=Cooper |date=2019-05-15 |title=Fast Six: What's trending in IndyCar |url=https://www.indycar.com/News/2019/05/05-15-Fast-Six |access-date=2025-01-30 |website=IndyCar |archive-date=April 11, In 2003, the [[Champ Car World Series]] race at [[Road America]] was renamed the "Mario Andretti Grand Prix" after Andretti helped broker a deal to keep it on the CCWS calendar.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2003-04-24 |title=Road America back on |url=https://www.autosport.com/general/news/road-america-back-on-5029008/5029008/ |access-date=2025-01-31 |work=[[Autosport]] |publisher=[[Motorsport Network]] |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411194652/https://www.autosport.com/general/news/road-america-back-on-5029008/5029008/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Andretti has also been honored by the [[Vince Lombardi]] Cancer Foundation (2007)<ref name="AndrettiPBio">{{cite web |title=Mario Andretti |url=http://www.marioandretti.com/pdf/Awards-Honors-Inductions_Revised_1-22-19.pdf |access-date=July 30, 2021 |website=MarioAndretti.com |publisher= |archive-date=June 10, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220610152603/https://www.marioandretti.com/pdf/Awards-Honors-Inductions_Revised_1-22-19.pdf |url-status=live }}</ref> and the [[Simeone Foundation Automotive Museum|Simeone Foundation]] (2008).<ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |date=2008-11-12 |title=Mario Andretti Receives Inaugural Spirit of Competition Award |url=https://simeonemuseum.org/spirit-of-competition/spirit-of-competition-award-winner/mario-andretti-receives-inaugural-spirit-of-competition-award-2/ |access-date=2025-01-30 |website=Simeone Foundation Automotive Museum |language=en-US |archive-date=March 25, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250325055750/https://simeonemuseum.org/spirit-of-competition/spirit-of-competition-award-winner/mario-andretti-receives-inaugural-spirit-of-competition-award-2/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

On October 23, 2006, the Italian government made Andretti a ''Commendatore'' of the [[Order of Merit of the Italian Republic]] (OMRI), the most senior Italian order of merit, in honor of Andretti's racing career and commitment to his Italian heritage.<ref name="CCWScommendatore">{{cite web |date=October 24, 2006 |title=Legendary race car driver Mario Andretti received commendatore honor from the news|url=http://www.edit.hr/lavoce/2008/foto/esuli081004.pdf |newspaper=[[La Voce del Popolo]] |language=it |title=Mario Andretti, sindaco del Libero Comune di Montona in esilio |trans-title=Mario == Personal life ==
[[File:MarioAndrettiAndJohnAndrettiMay2007.jpg|thumb|Mario (left) with nephew [[John Andretti|John]] at the [[2007 Indianapolis 500]].]]
Andretti lives in [[Bushkill Township, Pennsylvania]], a suburb of [[Nazareth, Pennsylvania|Nazareth]], on an estate that he named "Villa Montona" in honor of his birthplace.<ref name="Reinhard–2016" /> His late wife Dee Ann (née Hoch)<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.indystar.com/story/sports/motor/2018/07/04/motorsports-matriarch-dee-ann-andretti-dead-mario-marco-indycar/758025002/|title=Motorsports matriarch Dee Ann Andretti dies at 76|agency=Associated Press|website=[[The Indianapolis Star]]|archive-date=April 14, 2021|access-date=July 9, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210414070804/https://www.indystar.com/story/sports/motor/2018/07/04/motorsports-matriarch-dee-ann-andretti-dead-mario-marco-indycar/758025002/|url-status=live}}</ref> was a native of Nazareth. They met when Dee Ann was teaching Andretti English in 1961.<ref name="ESPNclassic" /><ref>Spanish Grand Prix, Eric della Faille Photograph Collection, Revs Institute, [https://library.revsinstitute.org/digital/custom/single-image?id=42652&collection=p17257coll1 Revs Digital Library] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200919185638/https://library.revsinstitute.org/digital/custom/single-image?id=42652&collection=p17257coll1 |date=September 19, 2020 }}.</ref> They were married on November 25, 1961, and had three children ([[Michael Andretti|Michael]], [[Jeff Andretti|Jeff]], and Barbara) and seven grandchildren.<ref name="Personal life" /> Dee Ann died on July 2, 2018, following a heart attack.<ref>{{cite web | title=Dee Ann Andretti passes away | work=[[Racer (magazine)|Racer]] | date=July 3, 2018 | url=https://racer.com/2018/07/03/dee-ann-andretti-passes-away/ | access-date=July 8, 2018 | archive-date=January 16, 2021 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210116225644/https://racer.com/2018/07/03/dee-ann-andretti-passes-away/ | url-status=dead }}</ref>

=== Andretti racing family ===
{{Further|Michael Andretti|Jeff Andretti|John Andretti|Marco Andretti}}

Both of Mario Andretti's sons, [[Michael Andretti|Michael]] and [[Jeff Andretti|Jeff]], were auto racers. Michael joined [[Championship Auto Racing Teams|CART]] in 1983 and won the [[1991 CART PPG Indy Car World Series|1991]] title; he also finished second on five occasions.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Andretti |url=https://imsmuseum.org/fame_inductee/michael-andretti/ |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=IMS Museum |language=en-US |archive-date=March 22, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250322074725/https://imsmuseum.org/fame_inductee/michael-andretti/ |url-status=live }}</ref> He was U.S. [[Driver of the Year]] in 1991,<ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |date=1991-12-08 |title=Like Father... Michael Andretti Named Driver of the Year |url=https://www.chicagotribune.com/1991/12/08/like-father-michael-andretti-named-driver-of-the-year/ |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=Chicago Tribune |language=en-US}}</ref> and was third on the all-time IndyCar career wins list when he retired.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Andretti |url=https://www.mshf.com/hall-of-fame/inductees/michael-andretti.html |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=Motorsports Hall of Fame of America |archive-date=January 16, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250116121513/https://www.mshf.com/hall-of-fame/inductees/michael-andretti.html |url-status=live }}</ref> Jeff Andretti competed in CART from 1990 to 1994.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Jeff Andretti |url=https://www.racing-reference.info/driver/Jeff_Andretti/ |access-date=2025-01-30 |website=Racing-Reference |publisher=NASCAR Digital Media, LLC |language=en-US}}</ref> Mario's nephew [[John Andretti]] competed in CART and NASCAR, winning one CART race in 1991 and two NASCAR races in 1997 and 1999.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cain |first=Holly |date=2020-01-31 |title=John Andretti demonstrated versatility and kindness and won for the sport's legends |url=https://www.nascar.com/news-media/2020/01/31/nascar-john-andretti-demonstrated-versatility-kindness/ |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=NASCAR |language=en-US}}</ref> In addition, in 2006, Mario's grandson [[Marco Andretti|Marco]] won the Indy Racing League Rookie of the Year award<ref>{{Cite web |title=Rookie of the Year Award |url=https://www.indycar.com/Fan-Info/INDYCAR-101/Awards/Rookie-Award |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=IndyCar |archive-date=January 23, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250123171307/https://www.indycar.com/Fan-Info/INDYCAR-101/Awards/Rookie-Award |url-status=live }}</ref> and the [[Indianapolis 500 Rookie of the Year]] Award, as Mario, Michael, and Jeff had done before him.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-04-28 |title=Top 10 Indy 500s, No. 9: Sam Hornish Jr. beats Marco Andretti in race's first last-lap pass |url=https://www.nbcsports.com/motor-sports/news/top-10-indy-500s-no-10-2006-sam-hornish-jr-marco-andretti |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=NBC Sports |language=en-US |archive-date=March 6, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250306214743/https://www.nbcsports.com/motor-sports/news/top-10-indy-500s-no-10-2006-sam-hornish-jr-marco-andretti |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harris |first=Mike |date=2008-07-22 |title=Marco looks marvelous in Indy debut |url=https://www.columbiamissourian.com/sports/other_sports/marco-looks-marvelous-in-indy-debut/article_ce76292f-79c1-5e46-bb39-1627b430a5f5.html |access-date=2025-01-29 |website=Columbia Missourian |language=en |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411194702/https://www.columbiamissourian.com/sports/other_sports/marco-looks-marvelous-in-indy-debut/article_ce76292f-79c1-5e46-bb39-1627b430a5f5.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

During the 1991 CART season, the Andrettis became the first family to have four relatives compete in the same series.<ref name="ESPN" /> In addition, the Andrettis have competed as a team in endurance racing. Mario, Michael, and John finished 6th at the [[1988 24 Hours of Le Mans]].<ref name="8W" /> Mario, Michael, and Jeff finished 5th at the [[1991 24 Hours of Daytona|1991 Rolex 24 at Daytona]].<ref>{{cite web |title=IMSA 1991: 24 h Daytona |url=http://www.classicscars.com/wspr/results/imsa/imsa1991.html#1 |access-date=January 31, 2009 |publisher=ClassicCars |archive-date=December 14, 2007 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071214192224/http://www.classicscars.com/wspr/results/imsa/imsa1991.html#1 |url-status=live }}</ref>

=== Business ===
[[File:Pocono Raceway IndyCar returns announcment.JPG|thumb|Andretti (second from right) in 2011, announcing the return of [[Pocono Raceway]] to the [[IndyCar Series]] schedule]]
Following his retirement, Andretti has remained active in the racing community. He serves on the board of the [[Cadillac in Formula One|Cadillac Formula One team]],<ref name="GMF1PR">{{cite web |date=November 25, 2024 |title=General Motors launches Cadillac Formula 1 Team and power unit |url=https://news.gm.com/home.detail.html/Pages/news/us/en/2024/nov/1125-f1.html |access-date=November 25, 2024 |website=[[General Motors]] |archive-date=November 26, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241126112427/https://news.gm.com/home.detail.html/Pages/news/us/en/2024/nov/1125-f1.html |url-status=live }}</ref> which will join Formula One in {{f1|2026}}.<ref name="CadiF1">{{cite web |date=November 25, 2024 |title=Statement on General Motors application to join FIA Formula One World Championship in 2026 |url=https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article/statement-on-general-motors-application-to-join-fia-formula-one-world.69uoRF1uwHwXkr4efkpnhc |access-date=November 25, 2024 |website=[[Formula One]] |archive-date=November 25, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241125183206/https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article/statement-on-general-motors-application-to-join-fia-formula-one-world.69uoRF1uwHwXkr4efkpnhc |url-status=live }}</ref> Since 2012, Andretti has been the official ambassador for the [[Circuit of the Americas]] (COTA) and the [[United States Grand Prix]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Cooper |first=Adam |date=June 5, 2012 |title=Mario Andretti appointed COTA PR ambassador |url=https://www.autoweek.com/racing/formula-1/a1960231/mario-andretti-appointed-cota-pr-ambassador/ |access-date=July 30, 2021 |website=[[Autoweek]]}}</ref> In the media, Andretti test drives cars for ''[[Road & Track]]'' and ''[[Car and Driver]]'' magazines<ref name="C16" /> and has penned a racing column for the ''[[Indianapolis Star]]''.<ref name="Istria">{{cite web |title=Mario Andretti |url=http://www.istrianet.org/istria/illustri/andretti/index.htm |archive-url=https://archive.today/20070311001100/http://www.istrianet.org/istria/illustri/andretti/index.htm |archive-date=March 11, 2007 |access-date=July 27, 2007 |work=Istria on the Internet}}</ref> He also participated in the 2006 [[Bullrun Rally]] from New York to Los Angeles.<ref name="C16" />

Andretti's business interests extend beyond racing. When he retired at age 54, his personal fortune was estimated at $100 million.<ref name="Comiskey–2017" /> In 1995, Andretti and [[Joe Antonini]] saved a struggling [[Napa Valley AVA|Napa Valley]] vineyard and renamed it the [[Andretti Winery]].<ref name="Lawrence–2015" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Sylt |first=Christian |date=2017-08-15 |title=How Mario Andretti Honors His Roots |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/csylt/2017/08/09/how-mario-andretti-honors-his-roots/ |access-date=2025-01-31 |website=Forbes |language=en |archive-date=September 26, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250926012145/https://www.forbes.com/sites/csylt/2017/08/09/how-mario-andretti-honors-his-roots/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Andretti was interviewed about his winemaking activities for the documentary ''A State of Vine'' (2007).<ref>{{Cite web |last=Boone |first=Virginie |date=2007-08-05 |title=Documentary filmmakers take entertaining look at wine industry and the people and stories behind it |url=https://www.pressdemocrat.com/article/news/documentary-filmmakers-take-entertaining-look-at-wine-industry-and-the-peop-2/ |access-date=2025-01-24 |website=Santa Rosa Press Democrat |language=en-US |archive-date=November 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241130063600/https://www.pressdemocrat.com/article/news/documentary-filmmakers-take-entertaining-look-at-wine-industry-and-the-peop-2/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In 1997, he founded Andretti Petroleum, which owns a chain of gasoline stations and car washes in Northern California.<ref name="Lawrence–2015" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Lewis |first=Jackson |date=2018-02-06 |title=Andretti Taps Istobal for Car-Wash Solutions |url=https://www.cspdailynews.com/technologyservices/andretti-taps-istobal-car-wash-solutions |access-date=2025-01-26 |website=CSP Daily News |language=en |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411194557/https://www.cspdailynews.com/technologyservices/andretti-taps-istobal-car-wash-solutions |url-status=live }}</ref> He also owns a chain of [[karting|go-kart]] tracks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thorn |first=Cody |date=2024-11-14 |title=Q&A: Meet Mario Andretti, co-owner of Andretti Indoor |url-status=live }}</ref> He was the title character of several video games, including ''[[Mario Andretti's Racing Challenge]]'' (1991),<ref>{{cite news |author=May, Scott |date=February 1992 == Film and television appearances ==
Andretti has contributed to several racing films. He features in and partially narrates ''The Speed Merchants'' (1972), a documentary about the [[1972 World Sportscar Championship]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ernst |first=Kurt |date=2014-06-26 |title=Simeone Museum to present "The Speed Merchants" with director Michael Keyser |url=https://www.hemmings.com/stories/simeone-museum-to-present-the-speed-merchants-with-director-michael-keyser/ |access-date=2025-01-30 |website=Hemmings: The Online Automotive Marketplace |archive-date=January 30, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250130202705/https://www.hemmings.com/stories/simeone-museum-to-present-the-speed-merchants-with-director-michael-keyser/ |url-status=live }}</ref> in which Andretti's Ferrari won the constructors' championship.<ref name="Nye–2017" /> He also drove an IndyCar in the [[IMAX]] film ''[[Super Speedway]]'' (1996).<ref>{{Cite web |title=Super Speedway: the Making of a Motion Picture Classic |url=https://www.stephenlow.com/super-speedway-the-making-of/ |access-date=2025-01-24 |website=The Stephen Low Company |language=en-US |archive-date=January 22, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250122022034/https://www.stephenlow.com/super-speedway-the-making-of/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="O'Leary–2002" />{{rp||page=110}} He also appeared in the documentary ''[[Dust to Glory]]'' (2005), which discusses a race in which he served as grand marshal.<ref>{{cite web |last=Krefting |first=Kevin |title=Mario Andretti, the Racer's Racer |url=http://www.ensenadagazette.com/dustglo3_05.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101217072903/http://www.ensenadagazette.com/dustglo3_05.html |archive-date=December 17, 2010 |access-date=December 8, 2011 |publisher=[[SPEED TV]]}}</ref> In November 2015, he appeared on the first season of TV series ''[[Jay Leno's Garage]]'', driving Leno in multiple fast cars and talking about his racing career.<ref>{{cite news |date=November 17, 2015 |title=Mario Andretti on 'Jay Leno's Garage'; Carlos Munoz returning to Andretti Autosport |url=https://www.mcall.com/sports/auto-racing/mc-mario-andretti-jay-leno-20151117-story.html |access-date=July 30, 2021 |work=The Morning Call |archive-date=July 30, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210730145146/https://www.mcall.com/sports/auto-racing/mc-mario-andretti-jay-leno-20151117-story.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

Andretti has also made cameo or guest appearances in other media, generally associated with racing. Like many other IndyCar drivers, he guested on the television show ''[[Home Improvement (TV series)|Home Improvement]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Benbow |first=Dana Hunsinger |title=All those times the Indy 500 popped up |url=https://www.indystar.com/story/sports/motor/2016/05/09/all-those-times-indy-500-popped-up/83600838/ |access-date=2025-01-24 |website=[[The Indianapolis Star]] |language=en-US}}</ref> He cameoed in ''[[Bobby Deerfield]]'' (1977);<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bellingham |first=Tom |date=2016-08-02 |title=Six of the best: Unlikely F1 Movie cameos |url=https://www.redbull.com/us-en/six-of-the-best-unlikely-f1-movie-cameos |access-date=2025-01-26 |website=Red Bull |language=en-us |archive-date=January 21, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250121093404/https://www.redbull.com/us-en/six-of-the-best-unlikely-f1-movie-cameos |url-status=live }}</ref> [[Pixar]]'s ''[[Cars (film)|Cars]]'' (2006) (an animated film where he was represented by a sentient version of the [[Ford Fairlane (Americas)|Ford Fairlane]] in which he won the 1967 Daytona 500);<ref>{{cite magazine|last=Lawrence|first=Andrew|title=After winning racing's rarest trifecta, Mario Andretti's ventures are thriving|url=https://www.si.com/racing/2015/05/19/mario-andretti-petroleum-winery-profiles|magazine=Sports Illustrated|date=May 19, 2015|access-date=July 30, 2021|archive-date=July 30, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210730145145/https://www.si.com/racing/2015/05/19/mario-andretti-petroleum-winery-profiles|url-status=live}}</ref> and [[DreamWorks Animation|DreamWorks]]' ''[[Turbo (2013 film)|Turbo]]'' (2013) (where he voiced the traffic director at Indianapolis Motor Speedway).<ref>{{cite news|title=Listen for Mario Andretti's voice in the movie 'Turbo'|url=https://www.mcall.com/sports/mc-xpm-2013-07-16-mc-mario-andretti-movie-turbo-20130716-story.html|work=[[The Morning Call]]|date=July 16, 2013|access-date=July 30, 2021}}</ref>
